title: I want, I need — surviving a café
objectives: Say what you want or need
Recognize masculine vs feminine articles (el / la)

vocab.hint: exception — feminine word, but takes 'el' because it starts with stressed 'a'

grammar_notes: Every Spanish noun has a gender — masculine (el / un) or feminine (la / una). Russian has three (м/ж/ср), Spanish has two.
Often -o = masculine, -a = feminine, but there are exceptions (el agua, el día, la mano).
Tip: learn each noun WITH its article — 'el café', not just 'café'.
example_phrases: Un café, por favor. (A coffee, please.)
Quiero agua, gracias. (I want water, thanks.)
¿Tienes pan? — Sí, tengo. (Do you have bread? — Yes, I do.)
La cuenta, por favor. (The bill, please.)
